headline: Educators converge on the harness around coding agents, context, memory, evaluation, and system design, as the model itself recedes to a fraction of the outcome.
body: Cole Medin leads with Google's 50-page agentic engineering playbook, arguing the model drives only 10% of coding outcomes while the surrounding harness of context, tools, guardrails, and verification drives the other 90%, and that developer roles shift from conductor to orchestrator [0]. The AI Engineer talks reinforce this from multiple angles: Victor Savkin frames agents as talented engineers with amnesia that lack durable context and memory [1], Ishaan Sehgal centers agent identity on append-only session logs rather than models or frameworks [3], Angie Jones argues engineering value moves to system architecture while decomposition and state management persist [9], and Nishant Gupta presents continuous evaluation pipelines measuring tool use, planning, and drift over static benchmarks [8]. Google Cloud supplies the infrastructure layer with its Agent Development Kit, Model Context Protocol connectors, BigQuery and AlloyDB integrations, AI-native databases, and Spanner hybrid search for retrieval [7][11][12][13]. Separately, Mike Krieger of Anthropic Labs discusses government-forced removals of Fable and Mythos alongside a safety posture he frames as material, a thread echoed by less reliable claims of Fable 5's return [10][2].

cited_items.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
